[index.html](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26948668/index.html)

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="UTF-8">
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
<title>TEACH Toolkit – Teletherapy-Enabled Action for Caregivers at Home</title>
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Source+Serif+4:ital,opsz,wght@0,8..60,400;0,8..60,500;0,8..60,600;1,8..60,400;1,8..60,500&family=DM+Sans:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
<style>
 :root {
 --cream: #f0faf9;
 --warm-white: #f2fbfa;
 --parchment: #e0f4f2;
 --sand: #b8dbd8;
 --taupe: #7ab5b0;
 --teal: #1a7a6e;
 --teal-light: #2d9e90;
 --teal-dark: #126057;
 --sage: #2a8a7c;
 --sage-dark: #1e6e62;
 --midnight: #0d3d38;
 --charcoal: #1a4e48;
 --warm-gray: #3d6b65;
 --body-text: #1a2e2c;
 --light-text: #4a7570;
 --radius: 6px;
 --shadow-soft: 0 1px 12px rgba(42,36,32,0.07);
 --transition: all 0.25s ease;
 }

 *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }
 html { scroll-behavior: smooth; }

 body {
 font-family: 'Source Serif 4', 'Lora', Georgia, serif;
 background: var(--cream);
 color: var(--body-text);
 line-height: 1.8;
 font-size: 18px;
 overflow-x: hidden;
 }

 /* ── PROGRESS ── */
 #progress-bar {
 position: fixed; top: 0; left: 0; height: 2px;
 background: var(--teal-light); width: 0%; z-index: 1000; transition: width 0.1s;
 }

 /* ── NAV ── */
 nav {
 position: fixed; top: 2px; left: 0; right: 0;
 background: rgba(248,253,252,0.97);
 backdrop-filter: blur(10px);
 -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(10px);
 border-bottom: 1px solid var(--sand);
 z-index: 900; padding: 0 2.5rem;
 display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between;
 height: 58px;
 }
 .nav-logo {
 font-family: 'Source Serif 4', 'Lora', Georgia, serif;
 font-weight: 600; font-size: 1rem;
 color: var(--charcoal); text-decoration: none;
 letter-spacing: 0.01em;
 }
 .nav-logo span {
 color: var(--warm-gray); font-weight: 400; font-size: 0.75rem;
 display: block; line-height: 1.2;
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 letter-spacing: 0.02em;
 }
 .nav-links { display: flex; gap: 0; list-style: none; align-items: center; }
 .nav-links a {
 text-decoration: none; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500;
 color: var(--warm-gray); padding: 0.3rem 0.75rem; border-radius: 4px;
 transition: var(--transition); letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase;
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 }
 .nav-links a:hover { color: var(--teal); }
 .nav-links a.active { color: var(--teal); }
 .nav-menu-btn {
 display: none; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer;
 padding: 0.4rem; color: var(--charcoal);
 }
 .mobile-nav {
 display: none; position: fixed; top: 60px; left: 0; right: 0;
 background: #f2fbfa; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--sand);
 z-index: 899; padding: 1rem 2rem; flex-direction: column; gap: 0;
 }
 .mobile-nav.open { display: flex; }
 .mobile-nav a {
 text-decoration: none; font-size: 0.92rem; font-weight: 400;
 color: var(--warm-gray); padding: 0.65rem 0;
 border-bottom: 1px solid var(--parchment);
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 }
 .mobile-nav a:last-child { border-bottom: none; }

 /* ── LAYOUT ── */
 main { padding-top: 60px; }

 /* ── HERO ── */
 .hero { background: #0d4a44; color: var(--cream); position: relative; overflow: hidden; }
 .hero-image-wrap { position: relative; width: 100%; height: 400px; overflow: hidden; }
 .hero-image-wrap img {
 width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover; object-position: center 22%; opacity: 0.38;
 }
 .hero-image-wrap::after {
 content: ''; position: absolute; inset: 0;
 background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(13,74,68,0.2) 0%, rgba(13,74,68,0.92) 100%);
 }
 .hero-text {
 position: absolute; bottom: 0; left: 0; right: 0;
 padding: 2rem 3rem 3.5rem; z-index: 2;
 max-width: 820px;
 }
 .hero-kicker {
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 font-size: 0.7rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.16em;
 text-transform: uppercase; color: #7de8de;
 margin-bottom: 0.9rem; display: block;
 }
 .hero h1 {
 font-size: clamp(2rem, 4.5vw, 3.2rem); font-weight: 600;
 line-height: 1.12; margin-bottom: 0.6rem; letter-spacing: -0.01em;
 }
 .hero h1 em { font-style: italic; color: rgba(248,245,240,0.75); }
 .hero-subtitle {
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 font-size: 0.82rem; color: rgba(240,250,249,0.72);
 margin-bottom: 1.5rem; letter-spacing: 0.06em;
 text-transform: uppercase; font-weight: 400;
 }
 .hero-intro {
 font-size: 1.1rem; line-height: 1.7; color: rgba(240,250,249,0.9);
 font-weight: 400; max-width: 540px;
 }

 /* ── SECTION NAV ── */
 .section-nav {
 background: var(--parchment); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--sand);
 padding: 0 2.5rem; display: flex; gap: 0;
 overflow-x: auto; scrollbar-width: none;
 position: sticky; top: 60px; z-index: 800;
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 }
 .section-nav::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none; }
 .section-pill {
 display: inline-flex; align-items: center;
 text-decoration: none; font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 500;
 color: var(--warm-gray); padding: 0.85rem 1rem;
 white-space: nowrap; transition: var(--transition);
 letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase;
 border-bottom: 2px solid transparent;
 }
 .section-pill:hover { color: var(--teal); border-bottom-color: var(--teal); }

 /* ── CONTENT ── */
 .content-section { max-width: 680px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 4.5rem 2rem; }
 .section-divider { border: none; border-top: 1px solid var(--sand); margin: 0; }
 .section-alt { background: #f2fbfa; }

 .section-kicker {
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 font-size: 0.68rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.16em;
 text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--teal);
 margin-bottom: 0.9rem; display: block;
 }

 h2 {
 font-size: clamp(1.65rem, 3vw, 2.2rem); font-weight: 600;
 line-height: 1.2; color: var(--midnight); margin-bottom: 1.6rem;
 letter-spacing: -0.01em;
 }
 h3 {
 font-size: 1.18rem; font-weight: 600; color: var(--charcoal);
 margin-top: 3rem; margin-bottom: 0.7rem; letter-spacing: -0.005em;
 }
 p { margin-bottom: 1.2rem; line-height: 1.8; }
 .lead { font-size: 1.12rem; line-height: 1.75; color: var(--charcoal); margin-bottom: 1.75rem; }

 /* ── IMAGES ── */
 .toolkit-img {
 width: 100%; border-radius: 4px;
 margin: 2.5rem 0 0.4rem; display: block; object-fit: cover;
 box-shadow: var(--shadow-soft);
 }
 .toolkit-img-caption {
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 font-size: 0.78rem; color: var(--taupe);
 margin-bottom: 2rem; font-style: italic; line-height: 1.5;
 }

 /* ── PULLQUOTE ── */
 .pullquote {
 border-left: 2px solid var(--teal);
 padding: 0.25rem 0 0.25rem 1.5rem;
 margin: 2rem 0;
 }
 .pullquote p {
 font-size: 1.08rem; font-style: italic; color: var(--charcoal);
 margin-bottom: 0; line-height: 1.7;
 }
 .pullquote-sage { border-left-color: var(--sage); }

 /* ── CALLOUT ── */
 .callout {
 background: var(--parchment);
 border-radius: var(--radius);
 padding: 1.25rem 1.5rem; margin: 2rem 0;
 border: 1px solid var(--sand);
 }
 .callout-label {
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 font-size: 0.68rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.12em;
 text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--teal);
 margin-bottom: 0.5rem; display: block;
 }
 .callout-label-sage { color: var(--sage-dark); }
 .callout p { margin-bottom: 0; font-style: italic; }

 /* ── LISTS ── */
 .body-list {
 list-style: none; margin: 0.5rem 0 1.4rem 0;
 display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.45rem;
 padding-left: 0;
 }
 .body-list li {
 padding-left: 1.4rem; position: relative;
 font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.7;
 }
 .body-list li::before {
 content: '–'; position: absolute; left: 0;
 color: var(--teal); font-weight: 500;
 }
 .body-list.sage li::before { color: var(--sage); }

 /* ── SCENARIO ── */
 .scenario {
 background: var(--parchment); border-radius: var(--radius);
 padding: 1.25rem 1.5rem; margin: 1.75rem 0;
 border: 1px solid var(--sand);
 }
 .scenario-label {
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 font-size: 0.68rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.12em;
 text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--sage-dark);
 margin-bottom: 0.5rem; display: block;
 }
 .scenario p { margin-bottom: 0; font-style: italic; font-size: 0.98rem; }

 /* ── TABLE ── */
 .duo-table {
 width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; margin: 1.5rem 0;
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 font-size: 0.9rem;
 }
 .duo-table thead tr { border-bottom: 2px solid var(--charcoal); }
 .duo-table thead th {
 padding: 0.6rem 1rem; font-size: 0.68rem; font-weight: 700;
 letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase;
 text-align: left; color: var(--warm-gray);
 }
 .duo-table tbody tr { border-bottom: 1px solid var(--sand); }
 .duo-table tbody tr:last-child { border-bottom: none; }
 .duo-table td { padding: 0.8rem 1rem; line-height: 1.55; vertical-align: top; }
 .duo-table td:first-child { font-weight: 500; width: 46%; color: var(--charcoal); }

 /* ── BALANCE TABLE (inline, no grid) ── */
 .balance-table {
 width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; margin: 1.2rem 0;
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 font-size: 0.9rem;
 }
 .balance-table td { padding: 0.65rem 1rem; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--sand); line-height: 1.5; }
 .balance-table td:first-child { font-style: italic; color: var(--warm-gray); width: 55%; }
 .balance-table td:last-child { font-weight: 600; color: var(--charcoal); }
 .balance-table tr:last-child td { border-bottom: none; }

 /* ── THREE ACTIONS (plain, no cards) ── */
 .three-actions { margin: 1.5rem 0 2rem; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0; }
 .action-item {
 padding: 1.4rem 0;
 border-bottom: 1px solid var(--sand);
 }
 .action-item:first-child { border-top: 1px solid var(--sand); }
 .action-num {
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 font-size: 0.68rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.1em;
 text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--sage-dark); margin-bottom: 0.2rem;
 display: block;
 }
 .action-title {
 font-size: 1.08rem; font-weight: 600; color: var(--charcoal);
 margin-bottom: 0.4rem; display: block;
 }
 .action-body { font-size: 0.97rem; color: var(--body-text); margin-bottom: 0.4rem; }
 .action-example { font-size: 0.9rem; color: var(--light-text); font-style: italic; }

 /* ── DO/DON'T (plain, two-col prose) ── */
 .do-dont { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 2rem; margin: 1.75rem 0; }
 .do-col h4, .dont-col h4 {
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 font-size: 0.68rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.12em;
 text-transform: uppercase; margin-bottom: 0.75rem;
 }
 .do-col h4 { color: var(--sage-dark); }
 .dont-col h4 { color: var(--teal-dark); }
 .do-col ul, .dont-col ul {
 list-style: none; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.4rem;
 }
 .do-col li, .dont-col li {
 font-size: 0.93rem; line-height: 1.55;
 padding-left: 1.1rem; position: relative;
 }
 .do-col li::before { content: '✓'; position: absolute; left: 0; color: var(--sage); }
 .dont-col li::before { content: '✗'; position: absolute; left: 0; color: var(--teal); }

 /* ── REFLECT ── */
 .reflect-box { border-top: 1px solid var(--sand); border-bottom: 1px solid var(--sand); padding: 2rem 0; margin: 2.5rem 0; }
 .reflect-box h3 { margin-top: 0; }
 .reflect-label {
 display: block; font-size: 0.9rem; color: var(--warm-gray);
 margin-bottom: 0.4rem; font-style: italic;
 }
 .reflect-field {
 width: 100%; min-height: 68px;
 background: #f2fbfa; border: 1px solid var(--sand);
 border-radius: var(--radius); padding: 0.75rem 1rem;
 font-family: 'Source Serif 4', 'Lora', Georgia, serif;
 font-size: 0.95rem; color: var(--body-text); resize: vertical;
 transition: var(--transition); line-height: 1.6; margin-bottom: 1rem; display: block;
 }
 .reflect-field:focus { outline: none; border-color: var(--sage); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(106,144,128,0.15); }
 .reflect-field::placeholder { color: var(--taupe); }

 /* ── TIMELINE ── */
 .timeline { margin: 1.5rem 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; }
 .timeline-item { display: flex; gap: 1.5rem; }
 .timeline-marker { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; flex-shrink: 0; padding-top: 0.2rem; }
 .timeline-dot {
 width: 10px; height: 10px; border-radius: 50%;
 background: var(--teal); flex-shrink: 0; margin-top: 6px;
 }
 .timeline-line { width: 1px; flex: 1; min-height: 20px; background: var(--sand); margin: 6px 0; }
 .timeline-item:last-child .timeline-line { display: none; }
 .timeline-content { padding-bottom: 1.75rem; flex: 1; }
 .timeline-period {
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 font-size: 0.68rem; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.1em;
 text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--teal); margin-bottom: 0.15rem;
 display: block;
 }
 .timeline-content h4 { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; color: var(--charcoal); margin: 0 0 0.3rem; }
 .timeline-content p { font-size: 0.93rem; color: var(--light-text); margin-bottom: 0; }

 /* ── QUOTES (dark band) ── */
 .quote-section { background: #0f5a52; padding: 4rem 2.5rem; }
 .quote-section-inner { max-width: 680px; margin: 0 auto; }
 .quote-kicker {
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 font-size: 0.68rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.16em;
 text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--teal-light);
 margin-bottom: 2.5rem; display: block;
 }
 .quote-stack { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2rem; }
 .quote-item { padding-left: 1.5rem; border-left: 2px solid rgba(125,232,222,0.5); }
 .quote-item p {
 font-size: 1rem; font-style: italic;
 color: rgba(240,250,249,0.92); line-height: 1.72; margin-bottom: 0;
 }

 /* ── CONCLUSION ── */
 .conclusion { background: #0f5a52; color: var(--cream); padding: 5rem 2.5rem; }
 .conclusion-inner { max-width: 640px; margin: 0 auto; }
 .conclusion h2 { color: var(--cream); margin-bottom: 1.5rem; }
 .conclusion h3 { color: rgba(240,250,249,0.85); margin-top: 2.5rem; font-size: 1rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.06em; font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; }
 .conclusion p { color: rgba(240,250,249,0.88); }
 .conclusion .body-list li { color: rgba(240,250,249,0.88); }
 .conclusion .body-list li::before { color: var(--teal-light); }
 .conclusion-img {
 width: 100%; max-width: 440px; border-radius: 4px;
 margin: 2.5rem auto; display: block; object-fit: cover;
 opacity: 0.88; box-shadow: 0 4px 30px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);
 }
 .final-message {
 font-size: 1.25rem; font-style: italic; color: var(--teal-light);
 margin-top: 2.5rem; line-height: 1.6; border-top: 1px solid rgba(248,245,240,0.1);
 padding-top: 2rem;
 }

 /* ── FOOTER ── */
 footer {
 background: #0a3832; color: rgba(248,245,240,0.65);
 text-align: center; padding: 1.5rem 2rem; font-size: 0.78rem;
 letter-spacing: 0.04em;
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 }

 /* ── FADE IN ── */
 .fade-up { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(18px); transition: opacity 0.5s ease, transform 0.5s ease; }
 .fade-up.visible { opacity: 1; transform: translateY(0); }

 /* ── RESPONSIVE ── */
 @media (max-width: 720px) {
 .nav-links { display: none; }
 .nav-menu-btn { display: block; }
 .hero-text { padding: 2rem 1.5rem 2.5rem; }
 .hero-image-wrap { height: 320px; }
 .content-section { padding: 3rem 1.5rem; }
 .do-dont { grid-template-columns: 1fr; gap: 1.5rem; }
 .section-nav { padding: 0 1rem; }
 .quote-section { padding: 3rem 1.5rem; }
 .conclusion { padding: 3.5rem 1.5rem; }
 }
 @media print {
 nav, .section-nav, #progress-bar { display: none; }
 main { padding-top: 0; }
 .fade-up { opacity: 1; transform: none; }
 body { font-size: 12pt; }
 }

 .section-opener {
 background: #f2fbfa;
 border: 1px solid var(--sand);
 border-left: 3px solid var(--teal);
 border-radius: 0 var(--radius) var(--radius) 0;
 padding: 1.4rem 1.5rem;
 margin-bottom: 3rem;
 }
 .opener-label {
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 font-size: 0.68rem;
 font-weight: 700;
 letter-spacing: 0.12em;
 text-transform: uppercase;
 color: var(--teal);
 margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
 }
 .section-opener p {
 font-size: 0.95rem;
 color: var(--charcoal);
 margin-bottom: 0.75rem;
 font-style: italic;
 }
 .opener-list {
 list-style: none;
 padding: 0; margin: 0;
 display: flex;
 flex-direction: column;
 gap: 0.3rem;
 }
 .opener-list li {
 font-size: 0.88rem;
 color: var(--warm-gray);
 padding-left: 1rem;
 position: relative;
 line-height: 1.5;
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 }
 .opener-list li::before {
 content: '→';
 position: absolute;
 left: 0;
 color: var(--teal);
 font-size: 0.75rem;
 }
 .section-closer {
 background: var(--parchment);
 border: 1px solid var(--sand);
 border-radius: var(--radius);
 padding: 1.4rem 1.5rem;
 margin-top: 3rem;
 }
 .closer-label {
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 font-size: 0.68rem;
 font-weight: 700;
 letter-spacing: 0.12em;
 text-transform: uppercase;
 color: var(--sage-dark);
 margin-bottom: 0.75rem;
 }
 .closer-list {
 list-style: none;
 padding: 0; margin: 0;
 display: flex;
 flex-direction: column;
 gap: 0.5rem;
 }
 .closer-list li {
 font-size: 0.93rem;
 color: var(--body-text);
 padding-left: 1.1rem;
 position: relative;
 line-height: 1.55;
 }
 .closer-list li::before {
 content: '✓';
 position: absolute;
 left: 0;
 color: var(--sage);
 font-size: 0.8rem;
 }

  /* ── REFRAME BOXES ── */
  .reframe-box {
    border: 1px solid var(--sand);
    border-radius: 8px;
    overflow: hidden;
    margin: 1.5rem 0;
  }
  .reframe-row {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
  }
  .reframe-feel {
    background: #fff5f5;
    padding: 1rem 1.25rem;
    border-right: 1px solid var(--sand);
  }
  .reframe-actual {
    background: #f0faf9;
    padding: 1rem 1.25rem;
  }
  .reframe-label {
    display: block;
    font-family: 'DM Sans', sans-serif;
    font-size: 0.65rem;
    font-weight: 700;
    letter-spacing: 0.12em;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    margin-bottom: 0.35rem;
  }
  .reframe-feel .reframe-label { color: #c0392b; }
  .reframe-actual .reframe-label { color: var(--teal); }
  .reframe-feel, .reframe-actual {
    font-size: 0.95rem;
    line-height: 1.55;
    color: var(--body-text);
  }
  @media (max-width: 600px) {
    .reframe-row { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
    .reframe-feel { border-right: none; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--sand); }
  }
</style>
</head>
<body>

<div id="progress-bar"></div>

<nav id="top-nav">
 <span class="nav-logo" onclick="goToSection('intro')" style="cursor:pointer;">TEACH Toolkit<span>Teletherapy-Enabled Action for Caregivers at Home</span></span>
 <ul class="nav-links">
 <li><a onclick="goToSection('welcome')" style="cursor:pointer;">Welcome</a></li>
 <li><a onclick="goToSection('section1')" style="cursor:pointer;">Section 1</a></li>
 <li><a onclick="goToSection('section2')" style="cursor:pointer;">Section 2</a></li>
 <li><a onclick="goToSection('section3')" style="cursor:pointer;">Section 3</a></li>
 <li><a onclick="goToSection('section4')" style="cursor:pointer;">Section 4</a></li>
 <li><a onclick="goToSection('conclusion')" style="cursor:pointer;">Closing</a></li>
 </ul>
 <button class="nav-menu-btn" id="menu-btn" aria-label="Menu">
 <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="1.75" stroke-linecap="round"><line x1="3" y1="5" x2="17" y2="5"/><line x1="3" y1="10" x2="17" y2="10"/><line x1="3" y1="15" x2="17" y2="15"/></svg>
 </button>
</nav>

<div class="mobile-nav" id="mobile-nav">
 <a onclick="goToSection('sparknotes');closeMobileNav();" style="cursor:pointer;">Start Here</a>
 <a onclick="goToSection('welcome');closeMobileNav();" style="cursor:pointer;">Welcome</a>

<!-- HERO -->
<main>
<section class="hero" id="intro">
 <div class="hero-image-wrap">
 <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Caregiver and child drawing together during a telehealth session">
 </div>
 <div class="hero-text">
 <span class="hero-kicker">Caregiver Resource · Pediatric Telehealth OT</span>
 <h1>The TEACH Toolkit</h1>
 <p class="hero-subtitle">Teletherapy-Enabled Action for Caregivers at Home</p>
 <p class="hero-intro">You already matter more in your child's therapy than you probably realize. This toolkit is here to help you see that.</p>
 </div>
</section>

<div class="section-nav" role="navigation" aria-label="Jump to section">
 <a onclick="goToSection('sparknotes')" class="section-pill" style="cursor:pointer;">Start Here</a>
 <a onclick="goToSection('welcome')" class="section-pill" style="cursor:pointer;">Welcome</a>
 <a onclick="goToSection('section1')" class="section-pill" style="cursor:pointer;">1 – Understanding Your Role</a>
 <a onclick="goToSection('section2')" class="section-pill" style="cursor:pointer;">2 – You Already Have What You Need</a>
 <a onclick="goToSection('section3')" class="section-pill" style="cursor:pointer;">3 – You Are Not Alone</a>
 <a onclick="goToSection('section4')" class="section-pill" style="cursor:pointer;">4 – Feeling Calm &amp; Ready</a>
 <a onclick="goToSection('conclusion')" class="section-pill" style="cursor:pointer;">Moving Forward</a>

<div id="sparknotes" style="background: #0f5a52; color: var(--cream);">
<div class="content-section">
  <span class="section-kicker" style="color:#7de8de; letter-spacing:0.18em;">Start Here</span>
  <h2 style="color:#f0faf9; font-size:clamp(1.8rem,4vw,2.6rem); margin-bottom:1.2rem;">If you only read one thing, read this.</h2>

  <p style="color:rgba(240,250,249,0.88); font-size:1.1rem; max-width:580px; margin-bottom:2rem; line-height:1.75;">You are probably a little nervous. That is normal. Here is everything you need to know before your first session — in about two minutes.</p>

  <!-- How to Use -->
  <div class="fade-up" style="background:rgba(255,255,255,0.06); border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12); border-radius:8px; padding:1.25rem 1.5rem; margin-bottom:1.5rem; max-width:620px;">
    <div style="font-family:'DM Sans',sans-serif; font-size:0.7rem; font-weight:700; letter-spacing:0.14em; text-transform:uppercase; color:#7de8de; margin-bottom:0.75rem;">How to use this toolkit</div>
    <ul style="list-style:none; padding:0; margin:0; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.5rem;">
      <li style="font-size:0.92rem; color:rgba(240,250,249,0.85); padding-left:1.25rem; position:relative; line-height:1.6;"><span style="position:absolute;left:0;color:#7de8de;">→</span> Read <strong>Section 1</strong> before your first session — that is all you need to start</li>
      <li style="font-size:0.92rem; color:rgba(240,250,249,0.85); padding-left:1.25rem; position:relative; line-height:1.6;"><span style="position:absolute;left:0;color:#7de8de;">→</span> Come back to other sections when something feels hard or unclear</li>
      <li style="font-size:0.92rem; color:rgba(240,250,249,0.85); padding-left:1.25rem; position:relative; line-height:1.6;"><span style="position:absolute;left:0;color:#7de8de;">→</span> On a phone, look for the <strong>☰ menu icon</strong> at the top right — tap it to see all sections</li>
      <li style="font-size:0.92rem; color:rgba(240,250,249,0.85); padding-left:1.25rem; position:relative; line-height:1.6;"><span style="position:absolute;left:0;color:#7de8de;">→</span> On a computer or tablet, the section names are visible along the top — tap or click any one to jump there</li>
      <li style="font-size:0.92rem; color:rgba(240,250,249,0.85); padding-left:1.25rem; position:relative; line-height:1.6;"><span style="position:absolute;left:0;color:#7de8de;">→</span> You do not have to read in order — jump to whatever you need right now</li>
      <li style="font-size:0.92rem; color:rgba(240,250,249,0.85); padding-left:1.25rem; position:relative; line-height:1.6;"><span style="position:absolute;left:0;color:#7de8de;">→</span> <strong>Save this page</strong> — bookmark it or save the link so you can find it again easily</li>
      <li style="font-size:0.92rem; color:rgba(240,250,249,0.85); padding-left:1.25rem; position:relative; line-height:1.6;"><span style="position:absolute;left:0;color:#7de8de;">→</span> The Quick Reference card at the bottom is for hard moments — no reading required</li>
    </ul>
  </div>

  <!-- What You May Need Nearby -->
  <div class="fade-up" style="background:rgba(255,255,255,0.06); border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12); border-radius:8px; padding:1.25rem 1.5rem; margin-bottom:2rem; max-width:620px;">
    <div style="font-family:'DM Sans',sans-serif; font-size:0.7rem; font-weight:700; letter-spacing:0.14em; text-transform:uppercase; color:#7de8de; margin-bottom:0.75rem;">Have these ready</div>
    <ul style="list-style:none; padding:0; margin:0; display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:0.4rem;">
      <li style="font-size:0.92rem; color:rgba(240,250,249,0.85); padding-left:1.25rem; position:relative; line-height:1.6;"><span style="position:absolute;left:0;color:#7de8de;">–</span> A charged phone, tablet, or computer</li>
      <li style="font-size:0.92rem; color:rgba(240,250,249,0.85); padding-left:1.25rem; position:relative; line-height:1.6;"><span style="position:absolute;left:0;color:#7de8de;">–</span> A quiet-enough space — it does not have to be perfect</li>
      <li style="font-size:0.92rem; color:rgba(240,250,249,0.85); padding-left:1.25rem; position:relative; line-height:1.6;"><span style="position:absolute;left:0;color:#7de8de;">–</span> A few familiar toys or things your child likes</li>
      <li style="font-size:0.92rem; color:rgba(240,250,249,0.85); padding-left:1.25rem; position:relative; line-height:1.6;"><span style="position:absolute;left:0;color:#7de8de;">–</span> Anything your therapist asked you to have ready</li>
    </ul>
  </div>

  <div class="sn-grid fade-up">

    <div class="sn-block">
      <div class="sn-num">Section 1 — Read this first</div>
      <div class="sn-title">Your three jobs. That is it.</div>
      <ul class="sn-list">
        <li><strong>Say what you see.</strong> "He's getting frustrated." That is enough.</li>
        <li><strong>Join in.</strong> Pick up a toy and start. Your child will follow.</li>
        <li><strong>Stay close.</strong> Even if they wander. Just stay nearby.</li>
        <li>You are not going to get in the way — the therapist will work with whatever is happening</li>
      </ul>
    </div>

    <div class="sn-block">
      <div class="sn-num">Section 2 — Read when you doubt yourself</div>
      <div class="sn-title">You are already doing this.</div>
      <ul class="sn-list">
        <li>Calming your child down, finding what interests them, helping them transition — that is therapy</li>
        <li>Nobody in that session knows your child better than you do</li>
        <li>Your home is not a limitation — it is actually where the learning sticks best</li>
        <li>You do not need to learn anything new — you just need to notice what is already working</li>
      </ul>
    </div>

    <div class="sn-block">
      <div class="sn-num">Section 3</div>
      <div class="sn-title">Whatever you are feeling — other families feel it too</div>
      <ul class="sn-list">
        <li>Real sessions are loud, messy, and unpredictable — that is not a sign something is wrong</li>
        <li>Most families have no idea what they are doing at first</li>
        <li>Even a hard session gives your therapist useful information</li>
        <li>Progress usually shows up at home before it shows up in sessions</li>
      </ul>
    </div>

    <div class="sn-block">
      <div class="sn-num">Section 4</div>
      <div class="sn-title">For when things feel like too much</div>
      <ul class="sn-list">
        <li>You do not need everything calm and ready before you start</li>
        <li>When it feels like too much — stop, sit with your child, focus on one thing</li>
        <li>You are allowed to slow down, take a breath, and ask for a minute</li>
        <li>Small things add up — you do not have to fix everything at once</li>
      </ul>
    </div>

    <div class="sn-block sn-block-full">
      <div class="sn-num">No matter what happens in that session</div>
      <div class="sn-title">These things are always true.</div>
      <ul class="sn-list sn-list-two-col">
        <li>Showing up is enough to start</li>
        <li>Messy sessions still count</li>
        <li>You know your child better than anyone</li>
        <li>Being present matters more than doing it perfectly</li>
        <li>Hard moments give your therapist useful information</li>
        <li>You are not doing this alone</li>
      </ul>
    </div>

  </div>
</div>
</div>

<div id="welcome">
<div class="content-section">
 <span class="section-kicker">Welcome</span>
 <h2>Telehealth therapy may look different than you expected.</h2>

 <p class="lead fade-up">Instead of the therapist working alone with your child, you are part of the session. That is a good thing. This toolkit is here to help. It is not a training program and you are not expected to be the therapist. It is just here to help you feel more sure of yourself going in.</p>

 <p class="fade-up">You may read through this before your first session or come back to certain parts over time. You do not need to remember everything at once.</p>

 <div class="pullquote fade-up"><p>You already matter in your child's therapy more than you probably realize. This toolkit is here to help you see that.</p></div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">What Telehealth Actually Looks Like</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Telehealth is not a watered-down version of therapy. It is just therapy in a different place.</p>

 <p class="fade-up">Instead of a clinic, therapy happens at home — in the middle of your child's real life. That is not a limitation. It is actually how learning sticks best.</p>

 <p class="fade-up">Sessions are messy. Kids move around, get distracted, and wander off. That is completely normal. You are not just sitting there watching — you are part of it. And that is exactly how it is supposed to work.</p>

 <h3 class="fade-up">Your Role Matters</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Telehealth works when you and the therapist work together. Your therapist knows how to run a session. You know your child. That is the whole team.</p>

 <div class="pullquote pullquote-sage fade-up"><p>Your job is not to do therapy perfectly. It is to be there for your child.</p></div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">One More Thing Before You Start</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">This is not a training program. There is no test, no right way to do it, and nobody is grading you. It is not a replacement for your therapist either — it is just something to read when you want to feel more sure of yourself going in.</p>

 <p class="fade-up">You can read it in order, skip around, or come back to one section when something feels hard. Use it however it helps.</p>

  <p class="fade-up" style="font-size:0.95rem; color:var(--light-text); margin-top:1.5rem;">Before your first session, check the <a onclick="goToSection('sparknotes')" style="color:var(--teal); cursor:pointer; text-decoration:underline;">Start Here section</a> for a quick list of what to have nearby.</p>

</div>
</div>

<hr class="section-divider">

<!-- ══ SECTION 1 ══ -->
<div id="section1" class="section-alt">
<div class="content-section">
 <span class="section-kicker">Section One</span>
 <h2>Understanding Your Role</h2>

 
<div class="section-opener fade-up">
 <div class="opener-label">Before You Read</div>
 <p>Read this before your first session or any time your role feels unclear.</p>
 <ul class="opener-list">
 <li>The difference between your role and the therapist's role</li>
 <li>Three simple things you can do in any session</li>
 <li>What to do when things go off track</li>
 </ul>
</div>


 <p class="lead fade-up">The question most parents have going in is simple: what am I actually supposed to do?</p>

 <p class="fade-up">Your role is specific, and it is manageable. This section tells you exactly what it is.</p>

 <div class="callout fade-up"><span class="callout-label">The Clear Answer</span><p>You are a partner in the session — not a bystander, and not the therapist. Your job is to support your child while the therapist guides what to do.</p></div>

  <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">Try This</span><p>During your next session, pick just one thing to focus on — noticing what your child is doing, joining in, or staying close. You do not need to do all three at once.</p></div>

 <div class="reframe-box fade-up">
  <div class="reframe-row">
    <div class="reframe-feel"><span class="reframe-label">It might feel like</span>You are getting in the way</div>
    <div class="reframe-actual"><span class="reframe-label">What's actually happening</span>You are helping the therapist understand your child</div>
  </div>
</div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">What Each Person Does</h3>

 <table class="duo-table fade-up">
 <thead><tr><th>Caregiver Role</th><th>Therapist Role</th></tr></thead>
 <tbody>
 <tr><td>Knows the child best</td><td>Guides the session</td></tr>
 <tr><td>Helps your child stay involved</td><td>Tries things and adjusts</td></tr>
 <tr><td>Responds in the moment</td><td>Watches and changes course</td></tr>
 <tr><td>Shares observations</td><td>Uses their training and experience</td></tr>
 </tbody>
 </table>
 <p class="fade-up" style="color:var(--light-text); font-size:0.93rem; font-style:italic;">You each bring something different. Together it works.</p>

 <h3 class="fade-up">Three Simple Ways to Support Your Child</h3>
 <p class="fade-up">Most of what you do during telehealth comes down to three simple actions:</p>

 <div class="three-actions fade-up">
 <div class="action-item">
 <span class="action-num">1 – Narrate</span>
 <span class="action-title">Say what you are noticing.</span>
 <p class="action-body">Say what you see — out loud, in plain words. Your therapist uses what you notice to decide what to do next.</p>
 </div>
 <div class="action-item">
 <span class="action-num">2 – Model</span>
 <span class="action-title">Join the activity.</span>
 <p class="action-body">Pick up a toy, start the action, show interest. Your child is more likely to get involved when you are part of it.</p>
 <p class="action-example">If your child is not engaging, you might start the activity yourself – stacking blocks or drawing – so they can watch and join when they are ready.</p>
 </div>
 <div class="action-item">
 <span class="action-num">3 – Stay Engaged</span>
 <span class="action-title">Be present, even if things feel messy.</span>
 <p class="action-body">Your presence is what helps your child stay involved — not having the perfect response.</p>
 <p class="action-example">Even if your child wanders off, stay close and keep going. Do not pack it in just because they walked away.</p>
 </div>
 </div>

 <div class="fade-up" style="background:var(--teal-dark); color:white; border-radius:10px; padding:1.5rem 2rem; margin:2rem 0; text-align:center;">
  <p style="font-size:0.75rem; letter-spacing:0.14em; text-transform:uppercase; color:rgba(255,255,255,0.6); margin-bottom:0.75rem; font-family:'DM Sans',sans-serif;">Remember just these three things</p>
  <div style="display:flex; justify-content:center; gap:2rem; flex-wrap:wrap;">
    <div style="text-align:center;">
      <div style="font-size:1.6rem; font-weight:700; color:#7de8de;">1</div>
      <div style="font-size:1rem; font-weight:600; margin-top:0.25rem;">Narrate</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.85rem; color:rgba(255,255,255,0.7); margin-top:0.2rem;">Say what you see</div>
    </div>
    <div style="text-align:center;">
      <div style="font-size:1.6rem; font-weight:700; color:#7de8de;">2</div>
      <div style="font-size:1rem; font-weight:600; margin-top:0.25rem;">Model</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.85rem; color:rgba(255,255,255,0.7); margin-top:0.2rem;">Join in</div>
    </div>
    <div style="text-align:center;">
      <div style="font-size:1.6rem; font-weight:700; color:#7de8de;">3</div>
      <div style="font-size:1rem; font-weight:600; margin-top:0.25rem;">Stay Engaged</div>
      <div style="font-size:0.85rem; color:rgba(255,255,255,0.7); margin-top:0.2rem;">Stay close</div>
    </div>
  </div>
  <p style="font-size:0.85rem; color:rgba(255,255,255,0.6); margin-top:1rem; margin-bottom:0;">That is enough. You do not need to remember anything else.</p>
</div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">When to Step In and When to Step Back</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Here is a simple way to know when to step in and when to step back:</p>

 <table class="balance-table fade-up">
 <tbody>
 <tr><td>Child is stuck, upset, or disengaged</td><td>Step in</td></tr>
 <tr><td>Child is trying, exploring, or staying involved</td><td>Step back</td></tr>
 </tbody>
 </table>

 <p class="fade-up">This will shift throughout the session. You are adjusting moment to moment, not following a fixed rule.</p>

  <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">In the Moment, Ask Yourself</span><p>"Do they need help right now, or are they figuring it out?" That quick check is enough to guide what you do next.</p></div>



 <h3 class="fade-up">Flexibility and Fit</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Nothing is set in stone. If something does not quite fit, simplify it, try part of it, or skip it. What matters is how your child responds — not whether it was done exactly as described. If something is not working, say so. "This isn't working for us" or "Can we try a different way?" is not being difficult. It is part of the process. Your therapist will adjust with you.</p>

 <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">For Example</span><p>If the therapist suggests using blocks and your child keeps throwing them, try rolling a ball instead. Same goal, different way in.</p></div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">When a Session Goes Off Track</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Some sessions will not go as planned. Your child may refuse to take part, get upset, or move away. You do not need to fix everything. Stay with your child, help them settle, and tell the therapist what is happening. Staying with your child matters more than keeping the activity going. You do not need perfect timing or a perfectly engaged child. If you are paying attention and responding, you are doing it right.</p>

 <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Caregiver and teenager working together at a laptop during a telehealth session" class="toolkit-img fade-up">

 <div class="pullquote pullquote-sage fade-up"><p>You are not there to run things. You are there for your child. That is what makes this work.</p></div>

  <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">Start Small</span><p>If you are not sure where to begin, try just one of the three — notice, join in, or stay close — for a few minutes. That is enough.</p></div>

<p class="fade-up" style="font-size:1.1rem; font-weight:600; color:var(--teal-dark);">You are already doing more than you think.</p>

<div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">In the Moment, You Can Say</span><ul class="body-list"><li>"He's getting frustrated."</li><li>"She's losing interest."</li></ul></div>

  <h3 class="fade-up">What You Can Say During a Session</h3>

  <p class="fade-up">You do not need to say things perfectly. Simple comments help. You might say:</p>
  <ul class="body-list"><li>"He usually does better with this in the morning."</li><li>"She's getting upset – I'm going to give her a minute."</li><li>"This part is hard for him."</li><li>"This worked yesterday, but today it's not."</li></ul>
  <p class="fade-up">These kinds of comments help your therapist adjust right away.</p>

<div class="section-closer fade-up">
 <div class="closer-label">Key Takeaways</div>
 <ul class="closer-list">
 <li>Your role is to support your child – not to run the session</li>
 <li>Narrate, model, and stay present – that is enough</li>
 <li>You will not interrupt or get in the way</li>
 <li>If something does not fit, say so – your input shapes what happens next</li>
 </ul>
</div>


</div>
</div>

<hr class="section-divider">

<!-- ══ SECTION 4 ══ -->
<div id="section2" >
<div class="content-section">
 <span class="section-kicker">Section Two</span>
 <h2>You Already Have What You Need</h2>

 
<div class="section-opener fade-up">
 <div class="opener-label">Before You Read</div>
 <p>Everything you already do for your child every day is exactly what supports them in therapy. This section helps you see that.</p>
 <ul class="opener-list">
 <li>Why your everyday caregiving already matters</li>
 <li>What taking part actually looks like</li>
 <li>How to find the balance between stepping in and stepping back</li>
 </ul>
</div>


 <p class="lead fade-up">You already know more than you think. You know what helps your child, what sets them off, what works on a good day and what falls apart when they are tired or overwhelmed. You have been figuring that out since day one. Telehealth does not ask you to be different. It just gives you a place to use what you already know.</p>

 <p class="fade-up">Your everyday moments already matter. This section helps you see why.</p>

<div class="quote-section">
 <div class="quote-section-inner">
  <span class="quote-kicker">Real Family Experiences</span>
  <div class="quote-stack">
   <div class="quote-item fade-up"><p>Our son ignored the screen for the first few sessions. I kept thinking it wasn't working. Then one day he brought a toy over and showed it to the therapist. I realized he had been paying attention the whole time – just in his own way.</p></div>
   <div class="quote-item fade-up"><p>I used to apologize at the beginning of every session for the noise, the mess, or my daughter not sitting still. One day the therapist told me, "This is exactly what I need to see." That changed everything.</p></div>
   <div class="quote-item fade-up"><p>There were sessions where nothing seemed to work. Then a week later, something we had tried showed up during play. It didn't happen in the session – it happened after.</p></div>
  </div>
 </div>
</div>

 

 <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up" style="margin: 1.5rem 0;">
 <span class="callout-label">Try This Before Your Next Session</span>
 <p>Pick one thing you already do — like helping your child calm down or stay interested — and use it on purpose during the session. Just one thing. Notice what happens.</p>
 </div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">You Are Already Doing Therapy-Type Things</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Think about what you already do in a regular day. Every one of these is something therapists do in sessions too:</p>

 <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Father and child doing breathing exercises together during a telehealth session" class="toolkit-img fade-up">
 <p class="toolkit-img-caption fade-up">None of that is small. That is literally what therapists do. The only difference is they have a plan going in. You do it because you know your kid.</p>

 <ul class="body-list fade-up">
 <li>Helping your child calm down when they are upset — so they can stay involved</li>
 <li>Noticing when something is not working for them — so you can spot what is getting in the way</li>
 <li>Changing how you offer something when it is not working — so they can keep trying</li>
 <li>Helping them move from one thing to the next — so transitions do not fall apart</li>
 <li>Finding what gets them interested — so they want to keep going</li>
 </ul>

 <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">Try This</span><p>Before your next session, pick one thing from this list and do it on purpose. Just one. Notice what happens.</p></div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">Your Home Is Part of the Therapy</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Your home is not a compromise. It is actually the best place for this. Your child is surrounded by their own stuff, their own routines, the people they feel safe with. That is not a limitation — it is the whole point.</p>

 <p class="fade-up">What your child practices at home tends to stick. Things you work on together can show up later — at the dinner table, during play, or in the middle of a regular day. That is because the learning is happening in the same place your child actually lives. </p>

 <div class="scenario fade-up"><span class="scenario-label">What This Might Look Like</span><p>Your child starts to lose interest and moves away from the screen. Instead of pulling them back, you follow them and pick up a nearby toy. You play alongside them. The therapist keeps guiding while you help your child stay involved. Your child may walk away but stay nearby, listening while playing with something else. That still counts. Your job is to stay with them. It does not have to look like anything in particular.</p></div>

  <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">If You're Not Sure What to Do in the Moment</span><p>Follow your child and start playing with something nearby. You do not need to bring them back to the screen right away.</p></div>

 <div class="reframe-box fade-up">
  <div class="reframe-row">
    <div class="reframe-feel"><span class="reframe-label">It might feel like</span>You are not doing "real therapy"</div>
    <div class="reframe-actual"><span class="reframe-label">What's actually happening</span>This is how kids learn best — in real life, at home</div>
  </div>
</div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">Finding the Balance</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">You might wonder if you are helping too much – or not enough. A simple way to think about it:</p>

 <table class="balance-table fade-up">
 <tbody>
 <tr><td>If your child is stuck</td><td>Step in and help</td></tr>
 <tr><td>If your child is trying</td><td>Give them space to keep going</td></tr>
 <tr><td>If your child is getting frustrated</td><td>Adjust or step in</td></tr>
 </tbody>
 </table>

 <p class="fade-up">There is no perfect balance. It shifts from moment to moment. What matters most is that you are paying attention and responding.</p>

 <h3 class="fade-up">When You're Not Sure What to Do</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">There will be moments when you are not sure how to respond. In those moments, you can:</p>
 <ul class="body-list"><li>Pause and watch</li><li>Follow your child's lead</li><li>Try something and see what happens</li><li>Tell the therapist what you are noticing</li></ul>
 <p class="fade-up">You do not need to have the answer right away. The more you try something, see what happens, and adjust, the better you get at this.</p>

  <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">You Can Always Start Here</span><p>Sit near your child and wait. You do not need to do anything — just be close. That is often enough to bring them back.</p></div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">When Something Doesn't Work</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Something that worked last week might not work today. That is not failure — that is your child changing and growing. Every attempt teaches you something, including the ones that fall flat.</p>

 <h3 class="fade-up">What Progress Might Look Like</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Early progress is not always easy to see. It might look like:</p>
 <ul class="body-list"><li>Staying focused a little longer</li><li>Coming back to an activity after leaving</li><li>Trying something new once</li><li>Needing a little less help</li><li>Bouncing back from frustration more quickly</li></ul>
 <p class="fade-up">These small changes matter. They build over time.</p>

 <h3 class="fade-up">Take a Moment If You Want To</h3>
 <p style="color:var(--light-text); font-style:italic; font-size:0.95rem; margin-bottom:1.5rem;">No right answers. Just whatever comes to mind.</p>
 <label class="reflect-label">One thing I already do that helps my child take part:</label>
 <textarea class="reflect-field" placeholder="Write anything that comes to mind..."></textarea>
 <label class="reflect-label">One thing I've noticed about my child that could help during sessions:</label>
 <textarea class="reflect-field" placeholder="Write anything that comes to mind..."></textarea>
 </div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">This Is Where You Are Starting</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">You are not starting from scratch. You are starting with:</p>
 <ul class="body-list sage"><li>Knowledge of your child</li><li>Experience helping them through daily challenges</li><li>Things you do for your child without even thinking about them</li><li>A relationship your child trusts</li></ul>

 <div class="pullquote pullquote-sage fade-up"><p>Telehealth builds on that. You already have what you need to begin. The rest comes through experience, over time.</p></div>

<div class="section-closer fade-up">
 <div class="closer-label">Key Takeaways</div>
 <ul class="closer-list">
 <li>What you already do at home is exactly what supports your child in therapy</li>
 <li>Your home is not a limitation — it is where the best learning happens</li>
 <li>Progress shows up in small ways, often outside of sessions</li>
 <li>You do not need to get it right — you just need to stay present and keep trying</li>
 </ul>
</div>


</div>

<!-- ══ QUOTES ══ -->


<hr class="section-divider">

<!-- ══ SECTION 2 ══ -->
<div id="section3" class="section-alt">
<div class="content-section">
 <span class="section-kicker">Section Three</span>
 <h2>You Are Not Alone in This</h2>

 
<div class="section-opener fade-up">
 <div class="opener-label">Before You Read</div>
 <p>Read this before your first session so you know what to expect — or come back to it after a hard one. Either way, what you are going through is normal.</p>
 <ul class="opener-list">
 <li>What real telehealth sessions actually look like</li>
 <li>Why hard moments are still useful</li>
 <li>What progress tends to look like over time</li>
 </ul>
</div>


 <p class="lead fade-up">Sessions at home do not look like sessions in a clinic. They are louder, messier, and harder to predict. Your child will probably move around, ignore the screen at some point, and have moments where nothing goes to plan. That is not a problem. That is just what this looks like.</p>

 <p class="fade-up">Most families are surprised by this at first. Once you know what to expect, those moments are a lot easier to get through.</p>

 <p class="fade-up">This section is full of things that will probably make you think — oh, that is us too.</p>

 <h3 class="fade-up">What Your First Session Will Probably Feel Like</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Awkward. Uncertain. Like everyone is figuring it out in real time — because they are. Your child may not engage with the therapist at all. You may not know when to jump in or when to stay back. The therapist will be watching and adjusting the whole time. You do not need to perform. You just need to show up and stay present. That is genuinely enough for a first session.</p>

 <h3 class="fade-up">What Real Telehealth Sessions Look Like</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Real telehealth sessions do not look like a quiet clinic room. They often include:</p>
 <ul class="body-list"><li>Children moving in and out of the activity</li><li>Short attention spans and quick shifts between tasks</li><li>Avoiding the screen or not showing interest</li><li>Siblings, pets, or background noise</li><li>Moments where things do not go as planned</li></ul>

 <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Father watching son who is away from the screen during a telehealth session" class="toolkit-img fade-up">
 <p class="toolkit-img-caption fade-up">This does not mean anything is going wrong. This is what therapy looks like at home.</p>

 <div class="pullquote pullquote-sage fade-up"><p>Your therapist is not expecting perfect conditions. They are working with what is actually happening in your space.</p></div>

  <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">If Your Session Looks Like This, Keep Going</span><p>Stay nearby, keep interacting, and let the therapist adjust. You do not need to stop or restart.</p></div>



 <div class="reframe-box fade-up">
  <div class="reframe-row">
    <div class="reframe-feel"><span class="reframe-label">It might feel like</span>The session is not working</div>
    <div class="reframe-actual"><span class="reframe-label">What's actually happening</span>This is normal — sessions at home often look like this</div>
  </div>
</div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">Your Child Might Not Seem to Be Paying Attention — But They Probably Are</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Your child may not always look at the screen or follow along in a clear way. They may listen without looking, watch briefly and move away, or get involved through you instead of the therapist. Some children ignore the therapist at first or need time to warm up. That is all normal. Kids take in a lot more than it looks like. It still counts.</p>

 <h3 class="fade-up">It Can Be Easy to Compare</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">You might wonder if other families have smoother sessions or if your child is having a harder time than most. Most families are dealing with the same things — it just does not always look that way from the outside. You only see what is happening in your own home.</p>

 <h3 class="fade-up">Your Therapist Is Adjusting Too</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Your therapist is figuring things out too. They are learning how your child responds, what works in your space, what to try next. You are building this together. It is not a performance where someone grades you at the end.</p>

 <h3 class="fade-up">When Things Don't Go as Planned</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">There will be sessions where your child refuses to take part, the activity falls apart, you feel like nothing worked, or the session feels scattered. These moments are expected. They give useful information — about what your child needs and what to try differently next time. A session does not need to look smooth to be useful.</p>

 <div class="scenario fade-up"><span class="scenario-label">For Example</span><p>Your child might refuse to join an activity or move away from the screen. Instead of ending the session, you and the therapist might shift to something your child is already drawn to, using that moment to support their involvement in a different way.</p></div>

  <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">In That Moment</span><p>Stay with your child and focus on helping them settle. The activity can wait.</p></div>

  <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">In the Moment, You Can Say</span><ul class="body-list"><li>"He's not into this right now."</li><li>"Can we try something else?"</li></ul></div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">Messy Moments Are Meaningful</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">The messy moments are actually the most useful ones. When your child melts down, refuses, or checks out completely — that is real information. It shows the therapist exactly what your child is working through and what they need help with. Nothing is wasted.</p>

 <h3 class="fade-up">Not Every Session Will Look the Same</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Some sessions will feel smooth. Others may feel hard or uneven. One week your child is very engaged. The next week they may be hard to reach. This is normal. Progress does not move in a straight line.</p>

 <div class="scenario fade-up"><span class="scenario-label">For Example</span><p>One session your child might stay engaged the whole time. The next session they may struggle to take part at all. Both sessions can still be useful.</p></div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">What This Tends to Look Like Over Time</h3>
 <p class="fade-up">Every family's experience is different, but many follow a similar pattern:</p>

 <div class="timeline fade-up">
 <div class="timeline-item">
 <div class="timeline-marker"><div class="timeline-dot"></div><div class="timeline-line"></div></div>
 <div class="timeline-content">
 <span class="timeline-period">Weeks 1–2</span>
 <h4>Getting Oriented</h4>
 <p>Everything feels new. Sessions may feel uneven. Your child may not engage right away.</p>
 <p class="timeline-action"><strong>Focus on:</strong> Just show up and stay near your child. That is the whole job right now.</p>
 </div>
 </div>
 <div class="timeline-item">
 <div class="timeline-marker"><div class="timeline-dot"></div><div class="timeline-line"></div></div>
 <div class="timeline-content">
 <span class="timeline-period">Weeks 3–6</span>
 <h4>Finding Your Footing</h4>
 <p>You start to notice patterns. Some activities work better than others. Things become more predictable.</p>
 <p class="timeline-action"><strong>Focus on:</strong> After each session, name one thing that worked — even something small. Tell your therapist.</p>
 </div>
 </div>
 <div class="timeline-item">
 <div class="timeline-marker"><div class="timeline-dot"></div><div class="timeline-line"></div></div>
 <div class="timeline-content">
 <span class="timeline-period">After Week 6</span>
 <h4>Building Momentum</h4>
 <p>You feel more at ease responding in the moment. Your child shows more steady engagement. Things you practiced start to show up in daily life.</p>
 <p class="timeline-action"><strong>Focus on:</strong> Start pointing out what you notice at home between sessions. Those observations shape what happens next.</p>
 </div>
 </div>
 </div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">One Honest Truth About Progress</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Some weeks will feel like nothing is moving. Your child might seem less engaged than the week before. Every family hits those patches. They are normal and they do not last. Progress is not always visible right away. Sometimes it shows up later, outside of sessions, in small but real ways.</p>

 <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Two scenes of caregivers and children in everyday activities – a grocery store and walking together – showing progress that happens outside sessions" class="toolkit-img fade-up">

 <p class="fade-up">Every family starts out uncertain. Every family goes through trial and error. Every family sees slow improvement before they see obvious improvement.</p>

 <div class="pullquote fade-up"><p>You are not behind. You are right where you are supposed to be.</p></div>

  <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">After a Hard Session</span><p>Name one thing that still went okay — even something tiny. Your child showed up. You showed up. That counts. Tell your therapist what you noticed, even if it felt like nothing worked.</p></div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">You Are Not Doing This Wrong</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">There is no version of telehealth that needs perfect behavior or perfect conditions. If your sessions feel messy, cut short, or hard to predict – you are not doing it wrong. You are doing telehealth exactly as it is meant to happen – in everyday life.</p>

 
<div class="section-closer fade-up">
 <div class="closer-label">Key Takeaways</div>
 <ul class="closer-list">
 <li>Noise, movement, and scattered sessions are normal – not signs of failure</li>
 <li>Most families go through the same uncertainty in the beginning</li>
 <li>Hard sessions still give your therapist useful information</li>
 <li>Progress often shows up outside sessions before it shows up in them</li>
 </ul>
</div>


</div>
</div>

<hr class="section-divider">

<!-- ══ SECTION 3 ══ -->
<div id="section4">
<div class="content-section">
 <span class="section-kicker">Section Four</span>
 <h2>Feeling Calm and Ready</h2>

 
<div class="section-opener fade-up">
 <div class="opener-label">Before You Read</div>
 <p>This section is for the hard moments – before a session when you feel rushed or anxious, or during one when everything feels like too much. Come back to this one whenever you need it.</p>
 <ul class="opener-list">
 <li>How to start from wherever you are</li>
 <li>What to do when the session feels overwhelming</li>
 <li>Why small shifts matter more than big changes</li>
 </ul>
</div>


 <p class="lead fade-up">Hard moments will happen. Your child will get frustrated or check out. You will not always know what to do next. That is okay — and this section will help you handle it without feeling like everything is falling apart.</p>

 <h3 class="fade-up">When Everything Feels Unclear</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Stop trying to manage the session. Pause. Look at your child. What do they need right now — not the activity, just your child. Move closer if they seem unsettled. Say something simple. Let the therapist know what you see.</p>

 

 <h3 class="fade-up">Starting Where You Are</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Some days may feel rushed or hard before the session even begins. That is okay. You can start from wherever you are. Preparation can be simple — choose a comfortable space, keep a few familiar toys nearby, and make sure your device is charged. If sessions start in the middle of a busy moment, take a breath, let the therapist know you need a minute, and ease in. The session does not depend on everything feeling settled first.</p>

  <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">Simple Way to Begin</span><p>Sit with your child, pick up a toy, and let the therapist guide from there.</p></div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">If You Notice…</h3>

 <table class="balance-table fade-up">
 <tbody>
 <tr><td>Feeling rushed</td><td>Slow down and take a moment</td></tr>
 <tr><td>Child not ready</td><td>Start gradually</td></tr>
 <tr><td>Feeling overwhelmed</td><td>Simplify and focus on one thing</td></tr>
 <tr><td>Technology issues</td><td>Keep interacting with your child and reconnect when you can</td></tr>
 </tbody>
 </table>

 <h3 class="fade-up">What Matters for a Session to Work</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">For a session to count, you really only need three things: your child is there, you can hear the therapist, and you are present. That is it. Everything else is flexible.</p>

 <h3 class="fade-up">If Your Child Isn't Ready</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Your child may not always be ready when the session starts. They might be distracted, not interested, or need time to warm up. Sessions can ease in slowly. It is okay to start gradually rather than expecting full engagement right away.</p>

  <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">You Can Ease In</span><p>Start with something your child is already doing instead of trying to begin a new activity right away.</p></div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">If You Feel Anxious Before Starting</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">It is common to feel unsure about how the session will go, worried your child won't take part, or concerned about how things might look. A simple reset can help.</p>

  <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">Remember</span><p>You are allowed to slow down. You do not have to push through when things feel hard.</p></div>

 <div class="reframe-box fade-up">
  <div class="reframe-row">
    <div class="reframe-feel"><span class="reframe-label">It might feel like</span>You should be more prepared</div>
    <div class="reframe-actual"><span class="reframe-label">What's actually happening</span>You already know your child. That is the most important thing.</div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="callout fade-up"><span class="callout-label">Simple Reset</span><p>Pause. Take a few slow breaths. Remind yourself of one thing you already know about your child. You are building on what you already know – not starting over.</p></div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">During the Session</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Sessions will include movement, noise, and moments that catch you off guard. You are not there to stop any of it. You are there to stay present through it.</p>

 <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">If Things Go Sideways Mid-Session</span><ul class="body-list"><li>Move closer to your child — physical closeness helps more than words</li><li>Drop the activity and just be with them for a moment</li><li>Say what you see — "She's done, I'm going to give her a minute" — and let the therapist adjust</li></ul><p>You do not need to fix it. You just need to stay in the room.</p></div>

  <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">If Your Child Is Completely Dysregulated</span><p>Stop the session activity entirely. Do not try to redirect back to the screen. Focus only on your child — get down on their level, speak calmly, and help them feel safe first. Let the therapist know what is happening. A session that ends early because your child needed you is not a failed session. You did the most important thing.</p></div>

  <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">If You're Unsure What to Do Next</span><p>Move closer, say what you see, and pause. That gives your therapist what they need to guide you.</p></div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">If Everything Feels Like Too Much</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">If the session starts to feel like too much, sit with your child and focus on one simple thing.</p>

 

  <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">In the Moment, You Can Try</span><p>Sit next to your child. Take a breath. Start with something simple.</p></div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">Technology Does Not Have to Be Perfect</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">If the connection cuts out or things feel delayed, keep interacting with your child. Keep the activity going, reconnect when needed, and let the therapist know what is happening.</p>

 <h3 class="fade-up">You Are Allowed to Pause</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">You do not need to respond right away to everything. It is okay to pause before responding, take a moment to think, or slow things down. That pause often helps both you and your child.</p>

 <h3 class="fade-up">Small Shifts Make a Difference</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">You do not need a perfect setup to have a good session. Small things make a real difference:</p>
 <ul class="body-list sage"><li>Set your phone down two minutes before the session starts</li><li>Have one familiar toy already out before you open the laptop</li><li>Take three slow breaths before you click join</li><li>After the session, name one thing — anything — that went okay</li></ul>
 <p class="fade-up">These small adjustments build over time.</p>

  <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up"><span class="callout-label">Try Just One at a Time</span><p>You do not need to do all of these. Small changes build over time.</p></div>

 <h3 class="fade-up">This Is a Process</h3>

  <p class="fade-up">Getting comfortable with this takes time. After a few sessions, things start to feel more familiar. You start to notice what works. You feel steadier in the moment. There is no point where everything suddenly becomes perfect — it just gets more manageable over time.</p>

  <p class="fade-up">You are learning how to do something new in the middle of everyday life. It will not always feel smooth. But each session builds on the last. You are showing up. You are adjusting. You are supporting your child. That is what matters.</p>

  <div class="section-closer fade-up">
 <div class="closer-label">Key Takeaways</div>
 <ul class="closer-list">
 <li>You do not need everything settled before the session starts</li>
 <li>When things feel like too much – slow down, focus on your child, one moment at a time</li>
 <li>You are allowed to pause, breathe, and ask for a minute</li>
 <li>Comfort in telehealth builds slowly – each session adds to the last</li>
 </ul>
</div>


</div>
</div>

<hr class="section-divider">

<!-- ══ CONCLUSION ══ -->
<div id="conclusion" class="conclusion">
 <div class="conclusion-inner">
 <span class="section-kicker" style="color:var(--teal-light);">Moving Forward</span>
 <h2>You Know More Than You Think</h2>

 <p>If you started reading this feeling unsure, you are probably feeling a little steadier now. That is the point.</p>

 <p>Sessions will still surprise you. But you have a foundation now. You know your role, you know what to expect, and you know what to do when things get hard.</p>

 <p>You already know what to do. You just needed someone to point it out. Show up, pay attention, stay with your child. That is the job. Progress will come — usually in small ways, usually when you are not looking for it.</p>

 <h3>Your Role Has Always Been There</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">What you do during sessions — how you respond, support, and stay present — is what creates the conditions for your child to take part and learn. That is not a small thing. Your child does not need a perfect session. They need you there, paying attention, staying with them. You have been doing that their whole life.</p>

 <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Grandparents and grandchild joyfully building blocks together" class="conclusion-img fade-up">

 <h3>You Do Not Have to Have It All Figured Out</h3>

 <p>Progress is not a straight line. Some sessions will feel great. Others will not. That is how this works for every family. You do not have to have it figured out. You just have to keep showing up — and you are already doing that.</p>

 <h3>Use This Toolkit as Needed</h3>

 <p>You are not expected to remember everything in this toolkit. Come back to sections when something feels hard. Not sure where to look? Section 1 covers your role. Section 2 covers what you already do. Section 3 is for when sessions feel hard. Section 4 is for when you feel anxious or overwhelmed. Revisit ideas as your child grows or changes. Use it as a reminder when things feel uncertain. This is a resource you can return to over time.</p>

 <h3>You've Got This</h3>

 <p class="fade-up">Before you started reading this, you were already doing things that helped your child. You were calming them down, figuring out what works, noticing what does not. You were already showing up.</p>

 <p class="fade-up">Telehealth just gives that a name and a place to happen.</p>

 <p class="final-message">You are not on the outside of your child's therapy.<br>You are the reason it works.</p>

 <div class="callout callout-label-sage fade-up" style="margin-top:2rem;"><span class="callout-label">Not Sure Where to Start?</span><p>Read Section 1 before your first session. That is all you need.</p></div>
 </div>
</div>

<footer>
 TEACH Toolkit &nbsp;·&nbsp; Teletherapy-Enabled Action for Caregivers at Home &nbsp;·&nbsp; A Caregiver Resource for Pediatric Telehealth Occupational Therapy
