<!-- QUICK REFERENCE CARD -->
<div id="reference" style="background: var(--parchment); border-top: 1px solid var(--sand);">
<div class="content-section">
 <span class="section-kicker">Quick Reference</span>
 <h2>When You Need It Fast</h2>
 <p class="lead fade-up">You do not need to read through the whole toolkit in a hard moment. Here is what matters most.</p>

 <div class="ref-grid fade-up">

 <div class="ref-block">
 <div class="ref-heading">When things feel like they are falling apart</div>
 <ul class="ref-list">
 <li>Stay with your child</li>
 <li>You do not have to fix it</li>
 <li>Tell the therapist what you see</li>
 <li>This is what a real session looks like</li>
 </ul>
 </div>

 <div class="ref-block">
 <div class="ref-heading">When you are not sure if you are doing it right</div>
 <ul class="ref-list">
 <li>If you are present, you are doing it</li>
 <li>Messy sessions still count</li>
 <li>Hard moments give your therapist useful information</li>
 <li>You do not need to say or do things perfectly</li>
 </ul>
 </div>

 <div class="ref-block">
 <div class="ref-heading">When you wonder if telehealth is working</div>
 <ul class="ref-list">
 <li>Progress often shows up outside sessions first</li>
 <li>One hard week does not erase what came before</li>
 <li>Talk to your therapist about what you are noticing</li>
 <li>You are right where you are supposed to be</li>
 </ul>
 </div>

 <div class="ref-block">
 <div class="ref-heading">When your child won't engage</div>
 <ul class="ref-list">
 <li>Stay nearby and stay calm</li>
 <li>Follow their lead rather than pulling them back</li>
 <li>Indirect engagement still counts</li>
 <li>Let the therapist know what you are seeing</li>
 </ul>
 </div>

 <div class="ref-block">
 <div class="ref-heading">When you feel overwhelmed</div>
 <ul class="ref-list">
 <li>Pause and take a breath</li>
 <li>Focus on one thing: your child</li>
 <li>You are allowed to slow down</li>
 <li>Tell the therapist you need a moment</li>
 </ul>
 </div>

 <div class="ref-block">
 <div class="ref-heading">Always true</div>
 <ul class="ref-list">
 <li>Showing up is enough to start</li>
 <li>You know your child better than anyone in the room</li>
 <li>Your presence matters more than your technique</li>
 <li>You are not doing this alone</li>
 </ul>
 </div>

 </div>

</div>
</div>

<style>
 .ref-grid {
 display: grid;
 grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(280px, 1fr));
 gap: 0;
 margin: 2rem 0 0;
 border: 1px solid var(--sand);
 border-radius: var(--radius);
 overflow: hidden;
 }
 .ref-block {
 padding: 1.4rem 1.5rem;
 border-right: 1px solid var(--sand);
 border-bottom: 1px solid var(--sand);
 background: #f2fbfa;
 transition: var(--transition);
 }
 .ref-block:hover { background: var(--cream); }
 .ref-heading {
 font-family: 'DM Sans', 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif;
 font-size: 0.72rem;
 font-weight: 700;
 letter-spacing: 0.08em;
 text-transform: uppercase;
 color: var(--teal);
 margin-bottom: 0.85rem;
 line-height: 1.4;
 }
 .ref-list {
 list-style: none;
 padding: 0; margin: 0;
 display: flex;
 flex-direction: column;
 gap: 0.5rem;
 }
 .ref-list li {
 font-size: 0.95rem;
 line-height: 1.55;
 color: var(--body-text);
 padding-left: 1rem;
 position: relative;
 }
 .ref-list li::before {
 content: '–';
 position: absolute;
 left: 0;
 color: var(--taupe);
 }
 @media print {
 .ref-grid { grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); }
 .ref-block { break-inside: avoid; }
 }
 @media (max-width: 600px) {
 .ref-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
 }
</style>

<!-- APPENDICES -->
<div id="appendix" style="background: var(--cream); border-top: 3px solid var(--teal);">
<div class="content-section">
 <span class="section-kicker">Appendices</span>
 <h2 class="fade-up" style="color: var(--teal-dark); font-size: clamp(1.8rem,4vw,2.6rem); margin-bottom: 0.5rem;">Quick-Use Tools</h2>
 <p class="fade-up" style="color: var(--body-text); font-size: 1.05rem; max-width: 650px;">These resources are here whenever you need them. You do not have to read them in order or all at once.</p>

 <!-- APPENDIX A: SESSION PREP CHECKLIST -->
 <div id="appendix-a" style="margin-top: 3rem;">
 <h3 class="fade-up" style="color: var(--teal-dark); font-size: 1.4rem; border-bottom: 2px solid var(--sand); padding-bottom: 0.5rem; margin-bottom: 1.5rem;">Appendix A: Session Prep Checklist</h3>
 <p class="fade-up" style="color: var(--body-text); margin-bottom: 1.5rem;">Before your session, take a few minutes to work through this list.</p>

 <style>
 .checklist-group { margin-bottom: 1.75rem; }
 .checklist-group h4 { color: var(--teal); font-size: 1rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; margin-bottom: 0.75rem; }
 .checklist-item { display: flex; align-items: flex-start; gap: 0.75rem; margin-bottom: 0.6rem; font-size: 1rem; color: var(--body-text); line-height: 1.5; }
 .checklist-box { width: 18px; height: 18px; min-width: 18px; border: 2px solid var(--teal); border-radius: 3px; margin-top: 2px; cursor: pointer; background: white; }
 .checklist-box.checked { background: var(--teal); }
 @media print { .checklist-box { background: white !important; } }
 </style>

 <div class="checklist-group fade-up">
 <h4>Your space</h4>
 <div class="checklist-item"><div class="checklist-box" onclick="this.classList.toggle('checked')"></div><span>Quiet area with minimal background noise</span></div>
 <div class="checklist-item"><div class="checklist-box" onclick="this.classList.toggle('checked')"></div><span>Good lighting on your child's face</span></div>
 <div class="checklist-item"><div class="checklist-box" onclick="this.classList.toggle('checked')"></div><span>Camera at your child's eye level</span></div>
 <div class="checklist-item"><div class="checklist-box" onclick="this.classList.toggle('checked')"></div><span>Seating that keeps your child comfortable and visible</span></div>
 </div>

 <div class="checklist-group fade-up">
 <h4>Your technology</h4>
 <div class="checklist-item"><div class="checklist-box" onclick="this.classList.toggle('checked')"></div><span>Device is charged or plugged in</span></div>
 <div class="checklist-item"><div class="checklist-box" onclick="this.classList.toggle('checked')"></div><span>Internet connection is stable</span></div>
 <div class="checklist-item"><div class="checklist-box" onclick="this.classList.toggle('checked')"></div><span>Platform is open and tested (audio and video working)</span></div>
 <div class="checklist-item"><div class="checklist-box" onclick="this.classList.toggle('checked')"></div><span>Phone silenced or nearby for backup contact</span></div>
 </div>

 <div class="checklist-group fade-up">
 <h4>Your child</h4>
 <div class="checklist-item"><div class="checklist-box" onclick="this.classList.toggle('checked')"></div><span>Fed, rested, and not in the middle of a transition</span></div>
 <div class="checklist-item"><div class="checklist-box" onclick="this.classList.toggle('checked')"></div><span>Briefly told what is coming ("We have therapy in a few minutes")</span></div>
 <div class="checklist-item"><div class="checklist-box" onclick="this.classList.toggle('checked')"></div><span>Preferred items or comfort objects nearby if needed</span></div>
 </div>

 <div class="checklist-group fade-up">
 <h4>Materials</h4>
 <div class="checklist-item"><div class="checklist-box" onclick="this.classList.toggle('checked')"></div><span>Any items your therapist asked you to have ready</span></div>
 <div class="checklist-item"><div class="checklist-box" onclick="this.classList.toggle('checked')"></div><span>Backup activities in case your child needs a break</span></div>
 </div>
 </div>

 <!-- APPENDIX B: GLOSSARY -->
 <div id="appendix-b" style="margin-top: 3.5rem;">
 <h3 class="fade-up" style="color: var(--teal-dark); font-size: 1.4rem; border-bottom: 2px solid var(--sand); padding-bottom: 0.5rem; margin-bottom: 1.5rem;">Appendix B: Glossary</h3>

 <style>
 .glossary-entry { margin-bottom: 1.25rem; padding-bottom: 1.25rem; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--sand); }
 .glossary-entry:last-child { border-bottom: none; }
 .glossary-term { font-weight: 700; color: var(--teal-dark); font-size: 1rem; }
 .glossary-def { color: var(--body-text); font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.65; margin-top: 0.25rem; }
 </style>

 <div class="glossary-entry fade-up">
 <div class="glossary-term">Engagement</div>
 <div class="glossary-def">How your child is taking part in a session. Engagement does not always look the same — your child may be listening, watching, or processing even when it is not obvious.</div>
 </div>
 <div class="glossary-entry fade-up">
 <div class="glossary-term">Occupational therapy (OT)</div>
 <div class="glossary-def">A type of therapy focused on helping people participate in the activities that matter in daily life. For children, this often includes play, self-care, communication, and learning.</div>
 </div>
 <div class="glossary-entry fade-up">
 <div class="glossary-term">Pediatric</div>
 <div class="glossary-def">Relating to children and child development. When you see this word, it simply means the service or approach is designed specifically for children.</div>
 </div>
 <div class="glossary-entry fade-up">
 <div class="glossary-term">Role (caregiver role)</div>
 <div class="glossary-def">In telehealth, your role is active. Rather than sitting back while a therapist works with your child, you are present, engaged, and often the one carrying out activities with your child during and between sessions.</div>
 </div>
 <div class="glossary-entry fade-up">
 <div class="glossary-term">Sensory</div>
 <div class="glossary-def">Related to what your child takes in through their senses — touch, sound, movement, sight, and more. Sensory needs refer to the ways your child's nervous system responds to those inputs, which can affect how they feel and behave during sessions.</div>
 </div>
 <div class="glossary-entry fade-up">
 <div class="glossary-term">Session</div>
 <div class="glossary-def">A scheduled meeting with your child's therapist, held over video. Sessions are typically structured around your child's goals but are meant to be flexible and responsive to what is happening in real time.</div>
 </div>
 <div class="glossary-entry fade-up">
 <div class="glossary-term">Telehealth</div>
 <div class="glossary-def">Delivering health or therapy services using technology — most often video calls — rather than in person.</div>
 </div>
 <div class="glossary-entry fade-up">
 <div class="glossary-term">Teletherapy</div>
 <div class="glossary-def">Therapy provided through a telehealth platform. Your child's occupational therapy sessions are a form of teletherapy.</div>
 </div>
 <div class="glossary-entry fade-up">
 <div class="glossary-term">Therapist</div>
 <div class="glossary-def">The occupational therapist leading your child's sessions. In telehealth, your therapist guides and coaches you as much as they work directly with your child.</div>
 </div>
 </div>

 <!-- APPENDIX C: TROUBLESHOOTING -->
 <div id="appendix-c" style="margin-top: 3.5rem;">
 <h3 class="fade-up" style="color: var(--teal-dark); font-size: 1.4rem; border-bottom: 2px solid var(--sand); padding-bottom: 0.5rem; margin-bottom: 1.5rem;">Appendix C: When Things Go Wrong</h3>
 <p class="fade-up" style="color: var(--body-text); margin-bottom: 1.75rem; font-size: 1.05rem;">Problems happen. Here is what to try when they do.</p>

 <style>
 .trouble-group { margin-bottom: 2rem; }
 .trouble-group h4 { color: var(--teal); font-size: 1rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; margin-bottom: 1rem; }
 .trouble-item { background: white; border-left: 3px solid var(--teal-light); border-radius: 0 6px 6px 0; padding: 1rem 1.25rem; margin-bottom: 0.85rem; }
 .trouble-problem { font-weight: 700; color: var(--teal-dark); font-size: 0.95rem; margin-bottom: 0.35rem; }
 .trouble-solution { color: var(--body-text); font-size: 0.95rem; line-height: 1.6; }
 </style>

 <div class="trouble-group fade-up">
 <h4>Technology problems</h4>
 <div class="trouble-item">
 <div class="trouble-problem">Video keeps freezing or cutting out</div>
 <div class="trouble-solution">Turn your Wi-Fi off and back on, move closer to your router, or switch from Wi-Fi to cellular data if possible. Close other apps or browser tabs that may be using bandwidth.</div>
 </div>
 <div class="trouble-item">
 <div class="trouble-problem">Can't hear the therapist, or they can't hear you</div>
 <div class="trouble-solution">Check that your device volume is up and not muted. Check microphone permissions in your device settings. Try unplugging and replugging headphones if you are using them.</div>
 </div>
 <div class="trouble-item">
 <div class="trouble-problem">Session drops completely</div>
 <div class="trouble-solution">Rejoin using the same link. If that does not work, contact your therapist by phone or text right away.</div>
 </div>
 </div>

 <div class="trouble-group fade-up">
 <h4>Session problems</h4>
 <div class="trouble-item">
 <div class="trouble-problem">My child refuses to participate</div>
 <div class="trouble-solution">Do not force it. Let your therapist know what you are seeing. A short break, a preferred item, or shifting to observation mode often helps. One hard session does not undo progress.</div>
 </div>
 <div class="trouble-item">
 <div class="trouble-problem">My child is having a meltdown</div>
 <div class="trouble-solution">It is okay to step away from the screen. Your child's needs come first. Signal to your therapist and attend to your child. Sessions can restart or reschedule.</div>
 </div>
 <div class="trouble-item">
 <div class="trouble-problem">I feel lost about what I am supposed to be doing</div>
 <div class="trouble-solution">Ask your therapist to clarify your role before or during the session. "Can you show me what you would like me to do?" is always a reasonable question.</div>
 </div>
 </div>
 </div>

 <!-- APPENDIX D: REFLECTION PROMPTS -->
 <div id="appendix-d" style="margin-top: 3.5rem; margin-bottom: 2rem;">
 <h3 class="fade-up" style="color: var(--teal-dark); font-size: 1.4rem; border-bottom: 2px solid var(--sand); padding-bottom: 0.5rem; margin-bottom: 1.5rem;">Appendix D: Reflection Prompts</h3>
 <p class="fade-up" style="color: var(--body-text); margin-bottom: 1.75rem; font-size: 1.05rem;">Use these after sessions, whenever you want to slow down and notice what is happening. These prompts are optional — write as much or as little as feels useful.</p>

 <style>
 .prompt-card { background: white; border: 1px solid var(--sand); border-radius: 10px; padding: 1.5rem; margin-bottom: 1.25rem; }
 .prompt-label { font-size: 0.85rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.1em; color: var(--teal); font-weight: 700; margin-bottom: 0.6rem; }
 .prompt-question { color: var(--teal-dark); font-size: 1.05rem; font-weight: 600; margin-bottom: 0.75rem; line-height: 1.5; }
 .prompt-lines { border-top: 1px solid var(--sand); margin-top: 0.5rem; }
 .prompt-line { border-bottom: 1px solid #ddd; height: 2rem; margin-top: 0; }
 </style>

 <div class="prompt-card fade-up">
 <div class="prompt-label">After a session that felt hard</div>
 <div class="prompt-question">What got in the way today? Was it my child, the environment, the technology, or how I was feeling? What is one thing I could adjust next time?</div>
 <div class="prompt-lines">
 <div class="prompt-line"></div>
 <div class="prompt-line"></div>
 <div class="prompt-line"></div>
 </div>
 </div>

 <div class="prompt-card fade-up">
 <div class="prompt-label">After a session that felt good</div>
 <div class="prompt-question">What worked? What did I do that seemed to help my child? How did I feel going into it?</div>
 <div class="prompt-lines">
 <div class="prompt-line"></div>
 <div class="prompt-line"></div>
 <div class="prompt-line"></div>
 </div>
 </div>

 <div class="prompt-card fade-up">
 <div class="prompt-label">On my role</div>
 <div class="prompt-question">When do I feel most confident during sessions? When do I feel least confident? What would help me feel more prepared?</div>
 <div class="prompt-lines">
 <div class="prompt-line"></div>
 <div class="prompt-line"></div>
 <div class="prompt-line"></div>
 </div>
 </div>

 <div class="prompt-card fade-up">
 <div class="prompt-label">On progress</div>
 <div class="prompt-question">What have I noticed changing over time — in my child, in myself, in how sessions feel? What am I still hoping for?</div>
 <div class="prompt-lines">
 <div class="prompt-line"></div>
 <div class="prompt-line"></div>
 <div class="prompt-line"></div>
 </div>
 </div>
 </div>

</div>
</div>

</main>
<script>
 // Progress bar
 window.addEventListener('scroll', () => {
 const p = window.scrollY / (document.documentElement.scrollHeight - window.innerHeight) * 100;
 document.getElementById('progress-bar').style.width = p + '%';
 });

 // Mobile menu toggle
 document.getElementById('menu-btn').addEventListener('click', () => document.getElementById('mobile-nav').classList.toggle('open'));
 function closeMobileNav() { document.getElementById('mobile-nav').classList.remove('open'); }

 // Smooth scroll function — used by all nav onclick handlers
 function goToSection(id) {
 const target = document.getElementById(id);
 if (!target) return;
 const offset = 70;
 const top = target.getBoundingClientRect().top + window.scrollY - offset;
 window.scrollTo({ top: top, behavior: 'smooth' });
 }

 // Smooth scroll for any remaining internal anchor links
 document.addEventListener('click', function(e) {
 const link = e.target.closest('a[href^="#"]');
 if (!link) return;
 const id = link.getAttribute('href').slice(1);
 if (!id) return;
 const target = document.getElementById(id);
 if (!target) return;
 e.preventDefault();
 closeMobileNav();
 goToSection(id);
 });

 // Active nav highlight
 const navLinks = document.querySelectorAll('.nav-links a');
 document.querySelectorAll('[id]').forEach(s => new IntersectionObserver(entries => {
 entries.forEach(e => {
 if (e.isIntersecting) navLinks.forEach(l => { l.classList[l.getAttribute('href') === '#' + e.target.id ? 'add' : 'remove']('active'); });
 });
 }, { rootMargin: '-60px 0px -70% 0px' }).observe(s));

 // Fade-in animation
 const fo = new IntersectionObserver(entries => {
 entries.forEach(e => { if (e.isIntersecting) { setTimeout(() => e.target.classList.add('visible'), 50); fo.unobserve(e.target); } });
 }, { rootMargin: '0px 0px -50px 0px' });
 document.querySelectorAll('.fade-up').forEach(el => fo.observe(el));

 // Download / Save
 function downloadToolkit() {
 const html = document.documentElement.outerHTML;
 const blob = new Blob([html], { type: 'text/html' });
 const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
 const a = document.createElement('a');
 a.href = url;
 a.download = 'TEACH_Toolkit.html';
 a.click();
 URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
 }

 // Share via Web Share API (works on mobile — triggers native share sheet including text/iMessage)
 async function shareToolkit() {
 if (navigator.share) {
 try {
 await navigator.share({
 title: 'TEACH Toolkit – Caregiver Resource for Telehealth OT',
 text: 'A resource to help caregivers feel ready for pediatric telehealth occupational therapy sessions.',
 url: window.location.href
 });
 } catch(e) {
 if (e.name !== 'AbortError') fallbackShare();
 }
 } else {
 fallbackShare();
 }
 }

 function fallbackShare() {
 const url = window.location.href;
 const text = encodeURIComponent('TEACH Toolkit – a caregiver resource for telehealth OT: ' + url);
 // Try SMS on mobile, fallback to email
 const isMobile = /iPhone|iPad|Android/i.test(navigator.userAgent);
 if (isMobile) {
 window.location.href = 'sms:?body=' + text;
 } else {
 window.location.href = 'mailto:?subject=TEACH Toolkit&body=' + text;
 }
 }

 // Copy current page URL to clipboard
 function copyLink() {
 const url = window.location.href;
 navigator.clipboard.writeText(url).then(() => {
 const btn = document.getElementById('copy-btn');
 btn.textContent = '✓ Copied!';
 setTimeout(() => { btn.innerHTML = '&#128279; Copy Link'; }, 2000);
 }).catch(() => {
 // Fallback for older browsers
 const ta = document.createElement('textarea');
 ta.value = url;
 document.body.appendChild(ta);
 ta.select();
 document.execCommand('copy');
 document.body.removeChild(ta);
 const btn = document.getElementById('copy-btn');
 btn.textContent = '✓ Copied!';
 setTimeout(() => { btn.innerHTML = '&#128279; Copy Link'; }, 2000);
 });
 }
</script>

</body>
</html>
